page: Amazon Web Services c7gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors built on the 64-bit ARM64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz with a single thread per core, backed by 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance is integrated with the Nitro hypervisor and includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage alongside a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 20766.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 97919.69, both placing in the top tiers. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, demonstrated by a Passmark memory score of 3129.82 and read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 6196.84 GB/sec. The local NVMe storage and memory architecture support high-throughput operations, as evidenced by 100.83 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 71.78 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

This hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and local high-speed storage without the overhead of simultaneous multithreading. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it less suitable for memory-bound applications requiring massive datasets in RAM, but highly effective for compute-heavy tasks. Optimal workloads include software compilation, high-performance web serving, containerized microservices, and distributed database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 30 Gbps baseline network interface managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks reveal exceptional multi-core efficiency, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 20766.0 and a software compilation score of 58685.0. Memory performance is equally robust, with a Passmark memory score of 3129.82 and read-only cached memory bandwidth of 6196.84 GB/sec. This instance delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive and storage-reliant workloads. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, Redis databases, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.16xlarge features 64 ARM64 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD for high-performance compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core ARM64 instance featuring high-speed local NVMe storage and optimized multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz on AWS Graviton architecture
128.0 GB system memory and 3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core performance with top-tier software compilation and memory benchmarks
Well-suited for distributed databases, Redis operations, and high-throughput web serving
